---
title: 'Metabolite robustness stability assessment through models bootstrapping'
author: 
    - name: 'Marc Clos-Garcia'
      affiliation: 'Steno Diabetes Center Copenhagen, Denmark'
      email: 'marc.clos.garcia@regiong.dk'
date: last-modified
date-format: "D MMMM, YYYY"
format: 
    html:
        toc: true
        toc-depth: 3
        number-sections: true
        code-fold: true
        code-summary: 'Click to expand/collapse code'
        theme: cosmo
---

In this report, we assess the robustness and stability of the selected metabolites for dementia prediction using Elastic Net models. We perform bootstrapping of the models to evaluate how consistently each metabolite is selected across different resampled datasets. We only tested the models that were constructed considering age and gender as covariates.

```{python}
# import libraries
import os as os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

from sklearn.linear_model import ElasticNetCV
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.utils import resample
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, mean_squared_error

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
import seaborn as sns

import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# set random seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(235)
```

```{python}
os.getcwd()
models_dir = '../results/modelling/'
```

```{python}
df = pd.read_csv('../data/csf_ratios2.csv', sep=';', encoding='latin1')

df['TAU'] = StandardScaler().fit_transform(np.log(df['TAU']).values.reshape(-1,1)).ravel() 
df['PTAU'] = StandardScaler().fit_transform(np.log(df['PTAU']).values.reshape(-1,1)).ravel() 
df['ABETA42'] = StandardScaler().fit_transform(df['ABETA42'].values.reshape(-1,1)).ravel()

# Define the 15 metabolites
metabolites=['d.Glucose', 'gluc.erythitol', 'd.Galactose', 'gluc.sorbitol', 'D.....Xylose', 'D.....Ribofuranose', 'Sorbitol', 'L.....Arabitol', 'D.Threitol', 'meso.Erythritol', 'X3.Deoxy.erythro.pentitol', 'X1.5.Anhydrohexitol', 'Ribonic.acid', 'L.Threonic.acid', 'Glyceric.acid']
```


*Traditionally*, stable metabolites are defined according to their selection frequency across bootstrapped models. In this analysis, we define a metabolite as stable if it is selected in at least 70% of the bootstrapped models.

* Selection frequency > 0.7 → highly robust

* 0.5–0.7 → moderately stable

* < 0.3 → likely unstable

Metabolites consistently selected across bootstraps provide the most reliable biological signals.

# Booststrapping results

::: {.panel-tabset}

## tTau prediction
Metabolites stability (selection frequency) and mean contribution coefficients table:
```{python}
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'bootstrap_TAU.pkl'))
bootstrap_ttau = pd.read_pickle(res_path)

# Summarize selection frequencies
results_ttau = pd.DataFrame({
    'metabolite': bootstrap_ttau['metabolites'],
    'selection_frequency': bootstrap_ttau['selection_freq'],
    'mean_coef': bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'].mean(axis=0),
    'coef_lower_95': np.percentile(bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'], 2.5, axis=0),
    'coef_upper_95': np.percentile(bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'], 97.5, axis=0)
}).sort_values('selection_frequency', ascending=False)

print(results_ttau)
```

Histogram of frequency selection:

```{python}
# plot stability
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
sns.histplot(bootstrap_ttau['selection_freq'], bins=20)
plt.title("Distribution of selection frequencies (tTau)")
plt.xlabel("Selection frequency")
plt.show()
```

### Stable metabolites
As defined by frequency of selection >70%


```{python}
stable_ttau = results_ttau[results_ttau.selection_frequency > 0.70]
stable_ttau
```

Contributions to the model - some statistics on the bootstraps
```{python}
mean_coefs = bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'].mean(axis=0)
# Sort metabolites by absolute mean coefficient (descending)
sorted_idx = np.argsort(np.abs(mean_coefs))[::-1]
sorted_metabolites = [bootstrap_ttau['metabolites'][i] for i in sorted_idx]
sorted_coefs = bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'][:, sorted_idx]
# print(sorted_coefs)
# print(sorted_metabolites)


# plot the general contribution
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.boxplot(data=pd.DataFrame(sorted_coefs, columns=sorted_metabolites))
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
plt.axhline(y=0, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
plt.ylabel('Coefficient value')
plt.title("Bootstrap coefficient distributions – tTau (sorted by contribution)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Prediction scores for tTau across bootstraps

```{python}
# plot the prediction scores of all 1000 bootstrap runs
plt.figure(figsize=(7,5))
sns.histplot(bootstrap_ttau['r2_sample'], bins=20)
plt.title("Bootstrap distribution of R² – tTau model\nR² CV: {:.3f}".format(bootstrap_ttau['r2_cv']))
plt.xlabel("R²")
plt.show()

print("Mean R²:", np.mean(bootstrap_ttau['r2_sample']))
print("SD R²:", np.std(bootstrap_ttau['r2_sample']))

results_ttau.to_csv(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, "bootstrap_ttau_selection_frequencies.csv")), index=False)
np.save(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, "bootstrap_ttau_coefs.npy")), bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'])
```


```{python}
y_ttau = df['TAU']
# tTau Prediction intervals (95% CI) for each sample
pred_lower = np.percentile(bootstrap_ttau['pred_matrix'], 2.5, axis=0)
pred_upper = np.percentile(bootstrap_ttau['pred_matrix'], 97.5, axis=0)
pred_mean = bootstrap_ttau['pred_matrix'].mean(axis=0)

# Plot: Which samples have high prediction uncertainty?
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.scatter(y_ttau, pred_mean, c=bootstrap_ttau['prediction_sd'], cmap='viridis', alpha=0.6)
plt.colorbar(label='Prediction SD')

# Add perfect prediction line
min_val = min(y_ttau.min(), pred_mean.min())
max_val = max(y_ttau.max(), pred_mean.max())
plt.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], 'r--', label='Perfect prediction', linewidth=2)

plt.xlabel('True tTau')
plt.ylabel('Predicted tTau (mean)')
plt.title('Prediction Uncertainty Across Samples for the tTau bootstrap model')

# Stats box
stats_text = f'N = {len(y_ttau)}\nR² = {np.mean(bootstrap_ttau['r2_sample']):.2f}\nR² (CV) = {bootstrap_ttau['r2_cv']:.2f}'
plt.text(0.05, 0.95, stats_text, transform=plt.gca().transAxes, 
        verticalalignment='top', bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5),
        fontsize=10)

plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```



## pTau prediction
Metabolites stability (selection frequency) and mean contribution coefficients table:
```{python}
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'bootstrap_PTAU.pkl'))
bootstrap_ptau = pd.read_pickle(res_path)

# Summarize selection frequencies
results_ptau = pd.DataFrame({
    'metabolite': bootstrap_ptau['metabolites'],
    'selection_frequency': bootstrap_ptau['selection_freq'],
    'mean_coef': bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'].mean(axis=0),
    'coef_lower_95': np.percentile(bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'], 2.5, axis=0),
    'coef_upper_95': np.percentile(bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'], 97.5, axis=0)
}).sort_values('selection_frequency', ascending=False)

print(results_ptau)
```

Histogram of frequency selection:

```{python}
# plot stability
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
sns.histplot(bootstrap_ptau['selection_freq'], bins=20)
plt.title("Distribution of selection frequencies (pTau)")
plt.xlabel("Selection frequency")
plt.show()
```

### Stable metabolites
As defined by frequency of selection >70%


```{python}
stable_ptau = results_ptau[results_ptau.selection_frequency > 0.70]
stable_ptau
```

Contributions to the model - some statistics on the bootstraps
```{python}
mean_coefs = bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'].mean(axis=0)
# Sort metabolites by absolute mean coefficient (descending)
sorted_idx = np.argsort(np.abs(mean_coefs))[::-1]
sorted_metabolites = [bootstrap_ptau['metabolites'][i] for i in sorted_idx]
sorted_coefs = bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'][:, sorted_idx]
# print(sorted_coefs)
# print(sorted_metabolites)


# plot the general contribution
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.boxplot(data=pd.DataFrame(sorted_coefs, columns=sorted_metabolites))
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
plt.axhline(y=0, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
plt.ylabel('Coefficient value')
plt.title("Bootstrap coefficient distributions – pTau (sorted by contribution)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Prediction scores for pTau across bootstraps

```{python}
# plot the prediction scores of all 1000 bootstrap runs
plt.figure(figsize=(7,5))
sns.histplot(bootstrap_ptau['r2_sample'], bins=20)
plt.title("Bootstrap distribution of R² – pTau model\nR² CV: {:.3f}".format(bootstrap_ptau['r2_cv']))
plt.xlabel("R²")
plt.show()

print("Mean R²:", np.mean(bootstrap_ptau['r2_sample']))
print("SD R²:", np.std(bootstrap_ptau['r2_sample']))

results_ptau.to_csv(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, "bootstrap_ptau_selection_frequencies.csv")), index=False)
np.save(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, "bootstrap_ptau_coefs.npy")), bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'])
```


```{python}
y_ptau = df['PTAU']
# pTau Prediction intervals (95% CI) for each sample
pred_lower = np.percentile(bootstrap_ptau['pred_matrix'], 2.5, axis=0)
pred_upper = np.percentile(bootstrap_ptau['pred_matrix'], 97.5, axis=0)
pred_mean = bootstrap_ptau['pred_matrix'].mean(axis=0)

# Plot: Which samples have high prediction uncertainty?
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.scatter(y_ptau, pred_mean, c=bootstrap_ptau['prediction_sd'], cmap='viridis', alpha=0.6)
plt.colorbar(label='Prediction SD')

# Add perfect prediction line
min_val = min(y_ptau.min(), pred_mean.min())
max_val = max(y_ptau.max(), pred_mean.max())
plt.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], 'r--', label='Perfect prediction', linewidth=2)

plt.xlabel('True pTau')
plt.ylabel('Predicted pTau (mean)')
plt.title('Prediction Uncertainty Across Samples for the pTau bootstrap model')

# Stats box
stats_text = f'N = {len(y_ptau)}\nR² = {np.mean(bootstrap_ptau['r2_sample']):.2f}\nR² (CV) = {bootstrap_ptau['r2_cv']:.2f}'
plt.text(0.05, 0.95, stats_text, transform=plt.gca().transAxes, 
        verticalalignment='top', bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5),
        fontsize=10)

plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


## Aβ42 prediction
Metabolites stability (selection frequency) and mean contribution coefficients table:
```{python}
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'bootstrap_ABETA42.pkl'))
bootstrap_ab42 = pd.read_pickle(res_path)

# Summarize selection frequencies
results_ab42 = pd.DataFrame({
    'metabolite': bootstrap_ab42['metabolites'],
    'selection_frequency': bootstrap_ab42['selection_freq'],
    'mean_coef': bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'].mean(axis=0),
    'coef_lower_95': np.percentile(bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'], 2.5, axis=0),
    'coef_upper_95': np.percentile(bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'], 97.5, axis=0)
}).sort_values('selection_frequency', ascending=False)

print(results_ab42)
```

Histogram of frequency selection:

```{python}
# plot stability
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
sns.histplot(bootstrap_ab42['selection_freq'], bins=20)
plt.title("Distribution of selection frequencies (Aβ42)")
plt.xlabel("Selection frequency")
plt.show()
```

### Stable metabolites
As defined by frequency of selection >70%


```{python}
stable_ab42 = results_ab42[results_ab42.selection_frequency > 0.70]
stable_ab42
```

Contributions to the model - some statistics on the bootstraps
```{python}
mean_coefs = bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'].mean(axis=0)
# Sort metabolites by absolute mean coefficient (descending)
sorted_idx = np.argsort(np.abs(mean_coefs))[::-1]
sorted_metabolites = [bootstrap_ab42['metabolites'][i] for i in sorted_idx]
sorted_coefs = bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'][:, sorted_idx]
# print(sorted_coefs)
# print(sorted_metabolites)


# plot the general contribution
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.boxplot(data=pd.DataFrame(sorted_coefs, columns=sorted_metabolites))
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
plt.axhline(y=0, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
plt.ylabel('Coefficient value')
plt.title("Bootstrap coefficient distributions – Aβ42 (sorted by contribution)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Prediction scores for Aβ42 across bootstraps

```{python}
# plot the prediction scores of all 1000 bootstrap runs
plt.figure(figsize=(7,5))
sns.histplot(bootstrap_ab42['r2_sample'], bins=20)
plt.title("Bootstrap distribution of R² – Aβ42 model\nR² CV: {:.3f}".format(bootstrap_ab42['r2_cv']))
plt.xlabel("R²")
plt.show()

print("Mean R²:", np.mean(bootstrap_ab42['r2_sample']))
print("SD R²:", np.std(bootstrap_ab42['r2_sample']))

results_ab42.to_csv(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, "bootstrap_AB42_selection_frequencies.csv")), index=False)
np.save(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, "bootstrap_AB42_coefs.npy")), bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'])
```


```{python}
y_ab42 = df['ABETA42']
# Aβ42 Prediction intervals (95% CI) for each sample
pred_lower = np.percentile(bootstrap_ab42['pred_matrix'], 2.5, axis=0)
pred_upper = np.percentile(bootstrap_ab42['pred_matrix'], 97.5, axis=0)
pred_mean = bootstrap_ab42['pred_matrix'].mean(axis=0)

# Plot: Which samples have high prediction uncertainty?
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.scatter(y_ab42, pred_mean, c=bootstrap_ab42['prediction_sd'], cmap='viridis', alpha=0.6)
plt.colorbar(label='Prediction SD')

# Add perfect prediction line
min_val = min(y_ab42.min(), pred_mean.min())
max_val = max(y_ab42.max(), pred_mean.max())
plt.plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], 'r--', label='Perfect prediction', linewidth=2)

plt.xlabel('True Aβ42')
plt.ylabel('Predicted Aβ42 (mean)')
plt.title('Prediction Uncertainty Across Samples for the Aβ42 bootstrap model')

# Stats box
stats_text = f'N = {len(y_ab42)}\nR² = {np.mean(bootstrap_ab42['r2_sample']):.2f}\nR² (CV) = {bootstrap_ab42['r2_cv']:.2f}'
plt.text(0.05, 0.95, stats_text, transform=plt.gca().transAxes, 
        verticalalignment='top', bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5),
        fontsize=10)

plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

:::

# Summary results

```{python}
# prepare summary table for all three outcomes for plotting
stable_mets_plot = pd.concat([
    stable_ttau[['metabolite']],
    stable_ptau[['metabolite']],
    stable_ab42[['metabolite']]
]).drop_duplicates().reset_index(drop=True)

# Extract coefficients for stable metabolites across all three biomarkers
coefs_ttau_stable = bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'][:, [metabolites.index(m) for m in stable_mets_plot['metabolite']]]
coefs_ptau_stable = bootstrap_ptau['coef_matrix'][:, [metabolites.index(m) for m in stable_mets_plot['metabolite']]]
coefs_ab42_stable = bootstrap_ab42['coef_matrix'][:, [metabolites.index(m) for m in stable_mets_plot['metabolite']]]

# Combine into a single dataframe for visualization
coefs_data = []
for i, met in enumerate(stable_mets_plot['metabolite']):
    for b in range(len(bootstrap_ttau['coef_matrix'])):
        coefs_data.append({'metabolite': met, 'tTau': coefs_ttau_stable[b, i], 'pTau': coefs_ptau_stable[b, i], 'Aβ42': coefs_ab42_stable[b, i]})

coefs_df = pd.DataFrame(coefs_data)

```


```{python}

# Add a column for metabolite categories/groups (manual dictionary mapping)
metabolite_names = {
    'L.....Arabitol': 'L-(-)-Arabitol',
    'd.Galactose': 'd-Galactose',
    'D.Threitol': 'D-Threitol',
    'Ribonic.acid': 'Ribonic Acid',
    'd.Glucose': 'd-Glucose',
    'gluc.erythitol': 'd-Glucose / Erythritol',
    'L.Threonic.acid': 'L-Threonic Acid',
    'X3.Deoxy.erythro.pentitol': '3-Deoxy-Erythro-Pentitol',
    'D.....Ribofuranose': 'D-(-)-Ribofuranose',
    'D.....Xylose': 'D-(-)-Xylose',
    'Sorbitol': 'Sorbitol'
}

coefs_df['metabolite_names'] = coefs_df['metabolite'].map(metabolite_names)
# print(coefs_df)
```


```{python}
# Create visualization with metabolites on x-axis and biomarkers side-by-side
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))

mets = stable_mets_plot['metabolite'].values
biomarkers = ['tTau', 'pTau', 'Aβ42']
colors = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c']
stable_dfs = [stable_ttau, stable_ptau, stable_ab42]

x = np.arange(len(mets))
width = 0.25

for idx, biomarker in enumerate(biomarkers):
    positions = x + idx * width
    
    for i, met in enumerate(mets):
        data = coefs_df[coefs_df['metabolite'] == met][biomarker].values
        pos = positions[i]
        
        # Determine alpha based on whether metabolite is stable for this biomarker
        alpha = 1.0 if met in stable_dfs[idx]['metabolite'].values else 0.25
        
        bp = ax.boxplot([data], positions=[pos], widths=width*0.8, patch_artist=True,
                       boxprops=dict(facecolor=colors[idx], alpha=alpha),
                       medianprops=dict(color='black', linewidth=1.5))

ax.set_xlabel('Metabolite', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Coefficient Value', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Bootstrap Coefficient Distributions - Stable Metabolites Across Biomarkers', fontsize=13)
ax.set_xticks(x + width)

# Get the mapped names for x-axis labels
met_names = [coefs_df[coefs_df['metabolite'] == m]['metabolite_names'].iloc[0] for m in mets]
ax.set_xticklabels(met_names, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.axhline(y=0, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)

# Create custom legend
legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor=colors[i], alpha=1.0, label=biomarkers[i]) for i in range(len(biomarkers))]
ax.legend(handles=legend_elements)

plt.tight_layout()
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'StableMetabolites_boxplots.png'))
plt.savefig(res_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\Real vs predicted models saved as "StableMetabolites_boxplots.png"')
plt.show()

```


```{python}
# Create a 3-panel plot
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))

# Define your three variables
variables = [
    {'y_true': df['TAU'], 'bootstrap': bootstrap_ttau, 'name': 'tTau'},{'y_true': df['PTAU'], 'bootstrap': bootstrap_ptau, 'name': 'pTau'},
    {'y_true': df['ABETA42'], 'bootstrap': bootstrap_ab42, 'name': 'Aβ42'}
]

for idx, var in enumerate(variables):
    plt.sca(axes[idx])  # Set current axis
    
    # Calculate predictions
    pred_mean = var['bootstrap']['pred_matrix'].mean(axis=0)
    
    # Scatter plot
    scatter = axes[idx].scatter(var['y_true'], pred_mean, 
                                c=var['bootstrap']['prediction_sd'], 
                                cmap='viridis', alpha=0.6)
    plt.colorbar(scatter, ax=axes[idx], label='Prediction SD')
    
    # Perfect prediction line
    min_val = min(var['y_true'].min(), pred_mean.min())
    max_val = max(var['y_true'].max(), pred_mean.max())
    axes[idx].plot([min_val, max_val], [min_val, max_val], 'r--', 
                   label='Perfect prediction', linewidth=2)
    
    axes[idx].set_xlabel(f'True {var["name"]}')
    axes[idx].set_ylabel(f'Predicted {var["name"]} (mean)')
    axes[idx].set_title(f'{var["name"]} Bootstrap Model')
    
    # Stats box
    stats_text = f'N = {len(var["y_true"])}\nR² = {np.mean(var["bootstrap"]["r2_sample"]):.2f}\nR² (CV) = {var["bootstrap"]["r2_cv"]:.2f}'
    axes[idx].text(0.05, 0.95, stats_text, transform=axes[idx].transAxes, 
                   verticalalignment='top', 
                   bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5),
                   fontsize=9)
    
    axes[idx].legend()
    axes[idx].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
res_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), models_dir, 'PredictedvsReal_allpanels_bootstrap.png'))
plt.savefig(res_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print('\Real vs predicted models saved as "PredictedvsReal_allpanels_bootstrap.png"')
plt.show()
```
